title: iria tenuicula
intro: Iria tenuicula, also known as the 'dwarf firebush', is a flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to treat various health conditions. The plant is known to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues like bronchitis and pneumonia. Herbal preparations of Iria tenuicula include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions and wounds or internally to support the immune system.
